note: Automatic Updates builds on core Package Manager to update Drupal core, adds a cron/unattended runner, a config object, and pre-update validators plus readiness checks. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: How does the Automatic Updates module actually apply a Drupal core update without breaking the live site, and which core module does it depend on to do so?
evals.expected_output: It depends on core's Package Manager module; it stages the Composer change in a temporary sandbox copy of the site (via a SandboxManagerBase), then commits it back live, entering maintenance mode only briefly.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want cron to automatically apply only security releases of Drupal core. Which config object and key do I set, and to what value?
evals.expected_output: Set unattended.level to 'security' on automatic_updates.settings (options: disable, security, patch). unattended.method controls web vs console.

evals.prompt: What is the difference between push-button and unattended updates in Automatic Updates, and what makes unattended updates run?
evals.expected_output: Push-button = the 'Update now' flow from the Available updates report (UpdateSandboxManager). Unattended = CronUpdateRunner, which decorates core's cron service and launches a detached background update after cron when unattended.level is not disable.

evals.prompt: Where do the 'Update readiness' status checks come from in Automatic Updates, and which service runs them?
evals.expected_output: The StatusChecker service (Validation\StatusChecker) runs the Package Manager validators outside an update, caches results, and surfaces them on the site Status report / admin messages; failures can be emailed during cron.

evals.prompt: Which permission gates the Automatic Updates 'Update now' and status pages, and does the module define its own permissions?
evals.expected_output: The core 'administer software updates' permission gates all its routes; the module defines no permissions of its own (no permissions.yml).

evals.prompt: Is the Automatic Updates contrib module a permanent add-on, and how does it relate to updating contributed modules and themes?
evals.expected_output: It is the Automatic Updates Initiative's contrib home and is slated to move into Drupal core. Contrib module/theme updates were handled by the Automatic Updates Extensions sub-module, now lifecycle: obsolete because that functionality moved into the main module.

evals.prompt: I want to add my own rule that blocks a core update unless a condition is met. How do I extend Automatic Updates / Package Manager to do this?
evals.expected_output: Register a Package Manager event-subscriber validator (like the module's VersionPolicyValidator, StagedProjectsValidator, etc.) subscribing to lifecycle events (PreCreate/PreApply/StatusCheck) and add ValidationResults; don't subclass the internal sandbox managers.
